message:
REVIEW: restore clippy + fmt gates broken by bd-yt66z

Commit 2c8fb4c (br-bd-yt66z) introduced three clippy errors and four
rustfmt diffs that trip `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D
warnings` and `cargo fmt --check` gates. Found during review-mode
cross-review of peer commits.

Clippy fixes in btrfs_resolve_inode_path_via_cache + assemble helper:

- significant_drop_tightening (14550): the Mutex guard on
  btrfs_inode_path_cache was held across walk_inode_ref_index. Scoped
  the guard in a block that returns an Arc<BtrfsInodePathIndex>, so
  the walk runs lock-free. OpenFs::btrfs_inode_path_cache becomes
  Mutex<Option<Arc<BtrfsInodePathIndex>>>. Concurrent INO_LOOKUP
  callers now proceed in parallel once the index has been populated;
  only the first-call rebuild path serialises on the mutex.
- unnecessary_map_or (14553): .map_or(true, ...) → .is_none_or(...).
- ptr_arg (14667): btrfs_assemble_ino_lookup_path takes &mut [Vec<u8>]
  instead of &mut Vec<Vec<u8>>; the body only invokes .reverse() and
  .iter(), both of which are slice methods.

Rustfmt fixes in the same commit: four wrapped expressions in
btrfs_build_inode_ref_index and the three new bd-yt66z tests.

Verification:
- rch cargo clippy -p ffs-core --lib --no-deps -- -D warnings: clean.
- rch rustfmt --check crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs: clean.
- rch cargo test -p ffs-core --lib: 632 passed, 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
